serving.natural_language_portion: One lemongrass stalk weighs approximately 60g total; only the pale white lower base (~10g) is edible and consumed; the fibrous green upper portion is discarded or used solely for flavouring.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Grass (Citronella), Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 167746)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 167746 reports nutrient values per 100g edible portion of raw lemongrass (citronella). Values used directly without adaptation. This is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (USDA Foundation Foods / SR Legacy).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index estimation for lemongrass — analogous low-GI aromatic herb/stalk reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Lemongrass is not directly listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~15 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) the food is a fibrous aromatic herb stalk with 25.3g carbohydrate per 100g edible portion, but the carbohydrate is predominantly structural (cell-wall polysaccharides) and largely indigestible; (2) at realistic culinary serving (~10g edible base per stalk), available carbohydrate is approximately 2.5g, yielding a glycemic load of ~0.04 — nutritionally negligible; (3) comparable low-GI aromatic herbs and stalks in the University of Sydney database consistently score GI ≤20. Confidence: 0.9. This estimation is documented per Project Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (6mg per 100g); negligible sodium at culinary dose (~0.6mg per 10g stalk base)
hypertension.guidance: Lemongrass contributes negligible sodium (~0.6mg per stalk base used in cooking) and is freely suitable for hypertensive patients; its aromatic intensity supports flavourful low-sodium cooking, reducing reliance on added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~15 (low); glycemic load ~0.04 per stalk at realistic culinary dose of 10g edible base
type2_diabetes.guidance: At a typical culinary serving of one stalk (~10g edible white base), lemongrass contributes approximately 2.5g available carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~0.04, producing no clinically meaningful glycaemic response and is freely suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1g per 100g), zero cholesterol, zero trans fat; potassium 723mg per 100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lemongrass contains negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and zero trans fat, with a favourable potassium content (723mg/100g); at culinary doses it poses no cardiovascular risk and supports heart-healthy cooking by enabling flavourful dishes without added salt or fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No pro-inflammatory nutrients at culinary doses; aromatic value reduces need for excess sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lemongrass provides no clinically significant pro-inflammatory nutrients at culinary doses and its aromatic properties support satisfying low-sodium cooking, which is beneficial for RA patients on corticosteroids who require sodium restriction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemongrass (Raw, Edible White Base) — Cymbopogon citratus — is a tall perennial grass native to Southeast Asia and South Asia, widely used as an aromatic flavouring ingredient in regional cuisines including Thai, Malaysian, Singaporean, Indonesian, and Vietnamese cooking. Only the pale green-white lower stalk base (~10g of a ~60g whole stalk) is edible and consumed; the fibrous green upper portion is discarded or used solely to infuse flavour into broths and curries. The edible base has a firm, slightly fibrous texture and a distinctive fresh citrus-lemon aroma derived primarily from citral, which constitutes approximately 75% of the essential oil. Per 100g edible white base (USDA SR Legacy FDC 167746): 99 kcal, 1.8g protein, 0.5g fat, 25.3g carbohydrate, 0g dietary fibre (the carbohydrate is predominantly structural cell-wall polysaccharide, largely indigestible), 6mg sodium, 723mg potassium, 65mg calcium, 8.2mg iron, 60mg magnesium, 75mcg folate. At a realistic culinary serving of one stalk (~10g edible base): approximately 10 kcal, 2.5g carbohydrate, 72mg potassium — nutritionally negligible. Lemongrass functions primarily as a FLAVOUR CARRIER rather than a nutrient source at culinary portions. GI ~15 (low); glycemic load ~0.04 per stalk. Freely suitable for diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management. Sold fresh at all Singapore wet markets and supermarkets. Also used as a tisane/herbal tea aromatic. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 167746. Confidence: high.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 167746 (exact match, approved primary source). GI is an analogous estimate with documented basis; confidence 0.9. All mandatory fields complete. No fabricated ontology codes.
notes: sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 167746 — lemongrass edible white base contains no free sugars at detectable levels. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by USDA SR Legacy for this entry; the carbohydrate fraction is predominantly structural cell-wall polysaccharide (largely indigestible) but is not captured as dietary fibre in the USDA SR Legacy analytical method for this entry — confirmed zero per source. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as lemongrass is a plant food. vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as lemongrass is a plant food with no B12. vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero as lemongrass is a plant food with no vitamin D. vitamin_e_mg is reported as 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero per source. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC 167746 for lemongrass. water_g set to -1: moisture content not separately reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry used. Per-100g values are provided for database completeness; at realistic culinary use (~10g edible white base per stalk), all nutrient contributions are nutritionally negligible. FoodOn URI not assigned — no specific FoodOn URI exists for lemongrass; closest_parent noted. SNOMED CT code not assigned — no code identified; not fabricated. FoodEx2 code A00UC used as closest_parent match.
